title: Rust Medium Path
description: Moderate Rust change (20–100 lines, 1–3 files). modum, self-review, PR. Stops at PR — user handles CI, review, merge.

tasks.title: Context Read
tasks.description: Read relevant Rust source code. Understand the problem, affected modules, function signatures, and callers. Check for existing test patterns.

tasks.title: Verify branch + rebase to origin/main
tasks.description:
Verify branch is fresh: `git branch -r | grep <branch-name>` — if it exists on any remote, rename (add `-v2`).

Fetch and rebase onto origin/main (NOT upstream/main — they have diverged):
```
git fetch origin
git rebase origin/main
```
Verify: `git diff origin/main --stat` shows ONLY the files you intend to change.
acceptance_criteria: branch name is unique across all remotes
git diff origin/main --stat shows only intended files

tasks.title: Implement
tasks.description: Write the code change. Keep it scoped to one issue — one logical change per PR. Do NOT bundle unrelated fixes. If you have multiple independent fixes, make separate branches and PRs.

tasks.title: modum check
tasks.description: Run `modum check --mode error`. Fix any violations before proceeding.

tasks.title: Self-review every changed line
tasks.description:
Read through the ENTIRE diff (`git diff origin/main`) line by line. Check:
- Logic errors: Does the control flow handle all edge cases? (early returns, None/Err paths, fallthroughs, type conversions)
- Unchanged call sites: Did you update ALL callers of a refactored function? Grep for old signatures — **this is the most common failure pattern**
- Message correctness: Do user-facing strings reference the right flags, paths, and commands?
- Test coverage: Do the tests actually test the new behavior, or just the old one with renamed variables? Does every new code path have a test?
- Edge cases: What happens when a dependency is missing, a subprocess fails, a config is empty?
- Consistency: Are variable names, function signatures, and arg ordering consistent across the change?
acceptance_criteria: all callers of changed functions updated

tasks.title: Push + PR
tasks.description:
Verify diff is clean BEFORE pushing: `git diff origin/main --stat`. If it shows hundreds of files, you're based on the wrong branch. Start over:
```
git checkout origin/main -b fix/your-fix-v2
```
Do not try to fix a dirty branch by rebasing — start fresh.

Push to upstream (NOT origin): `git push upstream <branch>`

Create PR: `gh pr create --repo adolfousier/opencrabs --head leshchenko1979:<branch>`

Immediately verify PR diff: `gh pr diff --name-only` should match expected files. If it doesn't, close the PR and start over from origin/main.
